bottleneck.evidence: result.json per_shape: memory-bound (AI 31.8/63.2 << ridge), candidate 164.2/173.6us vs ref 176.5/183.4us; at decode M<=64 each expert owns only M rows so moe_align_block_size pads every expert block up to BLOCK_SIZE_M=128 => up to ~8x wasted padded-row work in both fp8 GEMMs

approaches.summary: Call sglang's own try_get_optimal_moe_config, override ONLY BLOCK_SIZE_M (gemm1+down cfg) to clamp(next_pow2(M),16,128), recompute moe_align_block_size, then call sglang's own _fused_moe_kernel_sequence with the reference's exact fp8 args
approaches.why: BLOCK_SIZE_M changes only the tile grid, not the per-output-element fp32 K-accumulation (BLOCK_SIZE_K untouched) so calc_diff==0.0 (bit-exact) while removing padded-row work

approaches.summary: dequant->fp32 matmul gemm1 + silu_and_mul + a2-quant + gemm2
approaches.why: fp8 intermediate saturates (act amax ~76288 vs FP8_MAX 224, ~45% of values clamp at +/-224) so any tiny gemm1/quant diff flips many fp8 codes: feeding the reference's exact intermediate still gave calc_diff 3.15e-2 (~3000x over the 5e-6 gate)

approaches.summary: Apply the same BLOCK_SIZE_M shrink for M>32
approaches.why: at M>32 padding is a smaller fraction and the shrunk tile loses occupancy; measured ~0.7% regression, so run() falls back to reference for M>32

lesson: For dense-degenerate fp8 MoE decode (top_k==num_experts, tiny M), do NOT re-implement the fp8 GEMMs (fp8 saturation cliff makes calc_diff<=5e-6 unreachable). Instead reuse the reference Triton kernels and override ONLY BLOCK_SIZE_M (the tile-grid knob, not BLOCK_SIZE_K) to match the per-expert row count and kill moe_align padding: bit-exact and 1.05-1.08x.
transfers_to: fp8 fused-MoE decode on gfx942 with small M and dense/near-dense routing
any Triton moe where moe_align_block_size padding dominates at small M
caveats: No benefit (slight regression) at M>32 where padding is a small fraction
Relies on sglang exposing try_get_optimal_moe_config + _fused_moe_kernel_sequence internals
Bit-exactness depends on BLOCK_SIZE_K staying fixed
